content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF GROSSFELD, DOUGHERTY & GROSSFELD
KIEFER PLAZA
3201 FLORIN-PERKINS ROAD, SUITE 300
P.O. BOX 276005
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95826-6005
TELEPHONE (916) 386-2700
FAX (916) 386-0749

SEP 27 11:16 AM '91
GOVERNMENTAL RELATIONS
LARRY MARGOLIS
LARRY D. McCONNELL

September 23, 1991

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

By this letter, we are requesting of you a formal Advice Letter addressing the following:

If a partner of a law firm is a public official within the meaning of Government Code Section 82048, and the law firm employs a registered lobbyist so as to make it a lobbying firm as defined in Government Code Section 82038.5, can the partner who is a public official continue to expense certain usual and customary expenses associated with the practice of law (including lunches, entertainment and travel) without running afoul of the restrictions on gifts to public officials contained in Article II of the Political Reform Act, Government Code Sections 86201, et seq.?

We are advised by Jeanne Pritchard, Chief of the Division of Technical Assistance, as conveyed to us through Marianne Kvasager, that the established policy of the FPPC is that those expenses associated with the practice of law and the partner’s position with the firm may continue to be paid by the firm regardless of its status as a lobbying firm and the partner’s status as a public official. This seems to us to comport with reason and practice. While we are happy to rely on the informal advice of the Division Chief, we felt the question may be recurring and deserving of a formal statement of interpretation from your office.

Thank you for your assistance in this matter. We have found Ms. Kvasager and many members of the FPPC staff to be particularly responsive and helpful in addressing our questions.

Sincerely,

GROSSFELD, DOUGHERTY & GROSSFELD

Margaret J. Khan
